title: Unable to run podman-system-service in Ubuntu 20.04
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I'm able to run podman in Ubuntu 20.04. Want to run docker compose according to documentation: https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/compose-kubernetes-podman





<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I'm able to run: `sudo systemctl enable --now podman.socket`

But when trying to do:
```
sudo curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock http://localhost/_ping
curl: (7) Couldn't connect to server
```
Searching in internet found this package: http://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/hirsute/en/man1/podman-system-service.1.html#examples

But not sure how to install it or how to proceed. 

**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

comments.body: Do you have podman-docker installed? Otherwise, the symlink at `/var/run/docker.sock` will not exist.
@mheon I have podman install, what do you mean with podman-docker?
This is happening to me too.

there is a package podman-docker in fedora, which link docker.sock to podman.sock,I'm not sure is this package avaiable in ubuntu 2004, but at least you can create the link by yourself.
```
$ cat /usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/podman-docker.conf
L+  /run/docker.sock   -    -    -     -   /run/podman/podman.sock
```

> there is a package podman-docker in fedora ... I'm not sure is this package avaiable in ubuntu 2004

The package is not available in official ubuntu repos neither in kubic:  
https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04/

There is a env called `DOCKER_HOST`[1], you can set it to podman socket address to override default docker socket path.
eg: 
```sh
export DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
```

-  [[1]](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/cli/#environment-variables)
are the answers suitable enough to close this?
Closing due to inactivity. Please reopen if needed.
